targets.description: Valid platform targets for kiit libraries

items.description: Kotlin / Java server-side JVM
Android via AAR
JavaScript / TypeScript via npm
iOS / macOS via XCFramework and SPM

stability.description: Valid stability levels for kiit libraries

items.description: Production ready. No breaking changes without a major version bump.
API may change. Opt-in via @ExperimentalKiitApi.
Not for consumer use. Annotated @InternalKiitApi.
Scheduled for removal. Migration notes in library README.

migration.description: Migration status for each library from current to desired state

items.description: Not yet started
Migration underway, not yet verified
Some aspects migrated, others still pending
Fully migrated and verified
Library removed, no longer part of kiit

docTags.description: Valid documentation marker tags used in Kotlin example source files

items.description: Imports the consumer must add to use the library
Additional imports used only within the example code
Primary usage examples for the library
Helper and support functions used by the examples
Expected console output, wrapped in Hugo highlight block

categories.description: All kiit library categories. Each category maps to a segment in the artifactId and Kotlin package name, except where omitCategoryInArtifact is true. Libraries and full metadata are defined in each category's definitionFile.

items.description: Foundational primitives depended on by other kiit libraries internally. Must have no kiit dependencies of their own. No category segment in artifactId or package name.
Supplemental utilities and libraries to a standard library, such as arguments parser, config file loading, environemnt selection.
Abstractions for infrastructure concerns. Interfaces, types, and in-memory implementations only. No vendor SDK dependencies.
Flow control and fault tolerance primitives. Libraries that protect systems from overload, cascading failure, and resource exhaustion.
Database access abstractions and opinionated implementations. Low-level SQL, repository pattern, and entity service layer.
Runnable application templates and higher-level service libraries. Application base, background jobs, and RPC-style API server.
Concrete implementations of infra abstractions using real vendor SDKs. JVM only. Never depended on by other kiit libraries.
Connector libraries that wire two or more kiit libraries together.
Internal libraries used only within the kiit monorepo for build or test purposes. Never published externally.
